2603.26235.title: GS-BrainText: A Multi-Site Brain Imaging Report Dataset from Generation Scotland for Clinical Natural Language Processing Development and Validation
2603.26235.paper_summary: We present GS-BrainText, a curated dataset of 8,511 brain radiology reports from the Generation Scotland cohort, of which 2,431 are annotated for 24 brain disease phenotypes. This multi-site dataset spans five Scottish NHS health boards and includes broad age representation (mean age 58, median age 53), making it uniquely valuable for developing and evaluating generalisable clinical natural language processing (NLP) algorithms and tools. Expert annotations were performed by a multidisciplinary clinical team using an annotation schema, with 10-100% double annotation per NHS health board and rigorous quality assurance. Benchmark evaluation using EdIE-R, an existing rule-based NLP system developed in conjunction with the annotation schema, revealed some performance variation across health boards (F1: 86.13-98.13), phenotypes (F1: 22.22-100) and age groups (F1: 87.01-98.13), highlighting critical challenges in generalisation of NLP tools. The GS-BrainText dataset addresses a significant gap in available UK clinical text resources and provides a valuable resource for the study of linguistic variation, diagnostic uncertainty expression and the impact of data characteristics on NLP system performance.
2603.26235.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了 GS-BrainText，這是一個經過精心策劃的數據集，包含來自 Generation Scotland 隊列的 8,511 份腦部放射學報告，其中 2,431 份標註了 24 種腦部疾病表型。這個多地點數據集涵蓋了五個蘇格蘭 NHS 健康委員會，並且包括了廣泛的年齡代表性（平均年齡 58 歲，中位數年齡 53 歲），使其對於開發和評估可普遍應用的臨床自然語言處理（NLP）算法和工具具有獨特的價值。專家標註是由一個多學科的臨床團隊使用標註方案進行的，每個 NHS 健康委員會的重複標註率為 10-100%，並進行了嚴格的質量保證。使用 EdIE-R 進行基準評估，這是一個與標註方案共同開發的現有基於規則的 NLP 系統，顯示出各健康委員會之間的性能變異（F1: 86.13-98.13）、表型（F1: 22.22-100）和年齡組（F1: 87.01-98.13），突顯了 NLP 工具普遍化中的關鍵挑戰。GS-BrainText 數據集填補了英國臨床文本資源中的一個重要空白，並為語言變異、診斷不確定性表達以及數據特徵對 NLP 系統性能影響的研究提供了寶貴的資源。
2603.26235.author: Beatrice Alex et.al.
2603.26235.authors: Beatrice Alex, Claire Grover, Arlene Casey, Richard Tobin, Heather Whalley, William Whiteley
